DP.547.0002:
A small path through a lush, green,
wooded area. The densely grown trees
give off a thick aroma.
DP.547.0003:
A winding path that twists through
grassy fields. Young Trainers like to
test their battle skills here.
DP.547.0004:
Despite being so close to a big town,
this path retains its relaxed, natural
atmosphere.
DP.547.0005:
Once one gets through the tunnel,
the city of Oreburgh is just a stone’s
throw away.
DP.547.0006:
A charmingly natural path that wanders
past many ponds and groves of trees.
DP.547.0007:
The Ravaged Path was hewn through
a rocky outcropping. It is a shortcut
that leads straight to Floaroma Town.
DP.547.0008:
A wide, wooden walkway spans a large
pond. Fishermen idle their time away
dangling lines from the walkway.
DP.547.0009:
A quietly flowing stream and hilly
terrain with one-way ledges make this
a fun area for adventure.
DP.547.0010:
Near the entrance of Eterna Forest
is a cabin whose keepers offer rest
to weary travelers that stop by.
DP.547.0011:
Smoothly paved in asphalt, the Cycling
Road is heavenly for bicycle lovers.
DP.547.0012:
Below the Cycling Road, one can see
the entrance to Wayward Cave.
DP.547.0013:
Located at the foot of Mt. Coronet,
this road is carved into the rugged,
rocky terrain.
DP.547.0014:
A lush field of grass spreads from
Mt. Coronet’s sheer rock face,
creating a vista of contrasts.
DP.547.0015:
The Berry Master, an old man who loves
nature, Pokémon, and Berries, makes
his home here.
DP.547.0016:
The streams forded by this path wind
past copses and grassy patches in
a serene and soothing manner.
DP.547.0017:
The Lost Tower is where Pokémon are
laid to rest when their lives come
to an end.
DP.547.0018:
A path made by clearing an overgrown
canyon between mountains. It is always
shrouded by a dense fog.
DP.547.0019:
Grandma Wilma’s house stands alone in an
isolated spot deep among mountains.
DP.547.0020:
This narrow route is lined with deep,
tall grass that tickles the noses of
people straying off the path.
DP.547.0021:
The Cafe Cabin stands in a wood.
The house special is freshly milked
Moomoo Milk.
DP.547.0022:
A mountain path that tunnels through
Mt. Coronet, one of the tallest
mountains in Sinnoh.
DP.547.0023:
A tall, sturdy wall surrounds an
expansive estate that takes up nearly
half of the road space.
DP.547.0024:
Beyond the wall, one can see the
stately Pokémon Mansion and the
owner’s prized garden behind it.
DP.547.0025:
This path weaves among deep puddles
made by the unending rainfall. It is
wet and miserable for Trainers.
DP.547.0026:
There is a house here whose residents
offer to trade TMs for Red, Blue,
Yellow, or Green Shards.
DP.547.0027:
Offshore boulders form a jetty that
becalms waves reaching the beach.
A resort hotel overlooks the water.
DP.547.0028:
In a small house on the beach lives
a single man who refers to himself as
Dr. Footstep.
DP.547.0029:
The road joining Veilstone City and a
lake is described as either “wildly
natural” or simply “a mess.”
DP.547.0030:
There is a cave hewn into the sheer
rock wall at the roadside. The hole
was made by a Ruin Maniac.
DP.547.0031:
This area is always inundated by heavy
rainfall. Only hardy Trainers that can
take the rain gather here.
DP.547.0032:
This mountainous road leads from Mt.
Coronet. Its constant snowfall and
deep snowdrifts impede travelers.
DP.547.0033:
Snow blows down from Mt. Coronet and
grows into a harsh, ceaseless blizzard.
Be prepared for the worst.
DP.547.0034:
Despite its shortness, this road is
revered by fishing enthusiasts as a
great, yet little known, fishing spot.
DP.547.0035:
A beautiful white-sand beach only five
seconds on foot from Sandgem Town.
A pleasant breeze blows from the sea.
DP.547.0036:
The sea here is perfect for swimming,
with gentle winds and tides. There are
sandbars for resting, too.
DP.547.0037:
A straight path hemmed by green grass
and sparse stands of trees.
DP.547.0038:
At the end of the path stands the
gateway to Pal Park.
DP.547.0039:
A sandy beach extends from the road.
The beach is busy with avid Fishermen
happily casting at the water’s edge.
DP.547.0040:
A man who loves fishing lives near
here. There is also a man who will
compare sizes of caught fish.
DP.547.0041:
A marine route that requires travelers
to navigate around jutting rocks and
sandbars.
DP.547.0042:
With grass fields, rocky outcroppings,
the sea, and sandbars, this area is
like a miniature of the Sinnoh region.
DP.547.0043:
The path makes its way up and down
among rocky outcroppings. It is
physically challenging.
DP.547.0044:
A path that winds precariously along
sheer cliffs that go right to the edge
of the sea’s pounding waves.
DP.547.0045:
There is a small island in the bay.
An odd man who speaks foreign
languages lives there.
DP.547.0046:
A rugged and steep mountain path
where vision is limited by steadily
falling volcanic ash.
DP.547.0047:
This rough path is harshly raked by
a horizontally blowing sandstorm
driven by strong winds off the sea.
DP.547.0048:
A seaside path that makes its way
through wildly growing trees and
plants.
DP.547.0049:
A sea route that stretches from west
to east. There is an island that is
rich with plant life along the way.
DP.547.0050:
A straight path bounded by the sea on
both sides. It leads to the Flower
Paradise.
DP.547.0051:
A small town with the fresh scent
of new leaves in the air. It feels
like a place where adventures start.
DP.547.0052:
Your own home and your best friend’s
house are located here.
DP.547.0053:
A sandy town that is located right
next to a beach. It is redolent with
the salty scent of the sea.
DP.547.0054:
Prof. Rowan’s lab is the town’s
landmark. He conducts his
studies on Pokémon here.
DP.547.0055:
A town of flower lovers, Floaroma Town
is always perfumed with the sweet
scent of flowers.
DP.547.0056:
A short, floral pathway opens up to
a sprawling sea of flowers in bloom.
DP.547.0057:
In the center of town is the flower
shop called Pick a Peck of Colors.
DP.547.0058:
The temperate climate makes this town
a relaxed and casual place for people
and Pokémon to live in.
DP.547.0059:
There is a Pokémon Day Care and
a Pokémon Center here.
DP.547.0060:
Beyond a path hemmed by stands of
trees, one can find the entrance
to the Solaceon Ruins.
DP.547.0061:
A tiny town that preserves the history
of Sinnoh and the old ways of life.
DP.547.0062:
In the center of the town is a small
shrine that dates to ancient times.
DP.547.0063:
A city where hot-blooded Trainers
gather to work out and hone their
battling skills.
DP.547.0064:
A city that attracts Trainers who
know there are other ways of enjoying
Pokémon than battling.
DP.547.0065:
The most modernized city in the Sinnoh
region. It is bustling with people on
the go.
DP.547.0066:
The Pokétch Company, the developer
and manufacturer of the Pokétch, is
located here.
DP.547.0067:
Jubilife TV, Sinnoh’s TV network, is
located here. It is an entertaining
place to visit.
DP.547.0068:
The Global Trade Station--GTS for
short--is located here.
DP.547.0069:
Located here is the Trainers’ School.
Drop in to study up on the basics of
Pokémon.
DP.547.0070:
A port city that is bisected by a
canal. It has a distinctly exotic air
of foreign culture.
DP.547.0071:
Head to the library if you want a
workout for the mind. Head to the Gym
if you just want to work out.
DP.547.0072:
Large ships can always be seen
moored at the town’s piers.
DP.547.0073:
A vibrant and energetic mining town
that is blessed with a precious natural
resource.
DP.547.0074:
There is a Gym where mine workers
take breaks in between their
exhausting work shifts.
DP.547.0075:
There is a museum that specializes in
exhibits related to the town’s coal
mine.
DP.547.0076:
Huge deposits of coal still sleep
underneath the ground. The entrance
to the Oreburgh Coal Mine is here.
DP.547.0077:
An old city that shows fading and
almost-forgotten vestiges of ancient
history.
DP.547.0078:
The Cycle Shop is located here.
There is also a big building with
a slightly sinister atmosphere.
DP.547.0079:
A statue of an ancient Pokémon is
prominently and reverentially displayed.
DP.547.0080:
A Gym is indispensable in any town that
has many people coming and going.
This city is no exception.
DP.547.0081:
This friendly city started as a place
where people and Pokémon gathered,
then grew into a center of commerce.
DP.547.0082:
The entrance to Amity Square is here.
The square is a place where Trainers
can stroll peacefully with Pokémon.
DP.547.0083:
If you care about the looks of your
Pokémon, head for the Contest Hall.
For toughness, go to the Gym.
DP.547.0084:
There is a building here that is
symbolic of the city’s status as a
place where different cultures meet.
DP.547.0085:
A nice fountain with benches, as well
as the Pokémon Fan Club, are among
the city’s amenities.
DP.547.0086:
This city was originally founded to
protect the Great Marsh. It has grown
naturally over the years.
DP.547.0087:
The Gym is a good example of the
abundant water resources available to
the city.
DP.547.0088:
There is a lookout that affords views
of the Great Marsh. The gates to
the Safari Zone are also here.
DP.547.0089:
This city was made by carving out
steep, rocky mountains. Its isolation
limits its contact with other cities.
DP.547.0090:
There are warehouses for storing a
variety of goods. There is also a
major department store.
DP.547.0091:
At the highest point in the city is
Team Galactic’s headquarters.
DP.547.0092:
The city puts effort into providing
entertainment. Naturally, one can find
a Gym here.
DP.547.0093:
Conveniently located right next door
to the Game Corner is the Prize
Exchange.
DP.547.0094:
A port city that was built around the
bay portion of the cape. It is criss-
crossed by elevated walkways.
DP.547.0095:
The Gym is located atop a rocky
outcropping. Down on the beach is
a festive bazaar.
DP.547.0096:
The Vista Lighthouse’s bright beacon
illuminates the Sinnoh region.
DP.547.0097:
A winter wonderland of a city where
stout trees and buildings are blanketed
in thick snow.
DP.547.0098:
The Gym is at the heart of the city.
To the north is the Snowpoint Temple.
DP.547.0099:
Trainers seeking to become the best
arrive here after enduring a long and
grueling journey.
DP.547.0100:
The Pokémon League reception counter
is located here. Only those who have
beaten all the Gyms may enter.
DP.547.0101:
Victory Road is the last and toughest
of the challenges leading up to the
Pokémon League.
DP.547.0102:
A tiny port town where Trainers who
love battling more than eating gather.
DP.547.0103:
Moored at the pier is the ferry to
Snowpoint City.
DP.547.0104:
The gateway to the Battle Park is
located here.
DP.547.0105:
One of the lakes that is symbolic of
the water-rich Sinnoh region. There is
an odd legend associated with it.
DP.547.0106:
It is possible to enter the cave
underneath the lake from the
island in the middle.
DP.547.0107:
One of the lakes that is symbolic of
the water-rich Sinnoh region. There is
an odd legend associated with it.
DP.547.0108:
It is possible to enter the cave
underneath the lake from the
island in the middle.
DP.547.0109:
A high-class restaurant is located
on a hill overlooking the lake.
DP.547.0110:
One of the lakes that is symbolic of
the water-rich Sinnoh region. There is
an odd legend associated with it.
DP.547.0111:
It is possible to enter the cave
underneath the lake from the
island in the middle.
DP.547.0112:
The fourth lake of Sinnoh that was
kept secret.
DP.547.0113:
Beneath the lake’s surface is a cave
where dimensions are distorted.
DP.547.0114:
Numerous wind turbines stand in the
vicinity of the Valley Windworks.
DP.547.0115:
Winds blowing through the canyon feed
the wind turbines to generate clean
electricity.
DP.547.0116:
A forest enveloped in chilly air.
Thick stands of trees turn the forest
into a natural maze.
DP.547.0117:
Deep in the forest is the Old Chateau.
It is falling into disrepair after
being abandoned by its owner.
DP.547.0118:
The sprawling ironworks is located
with the forest at its back.
DP.547.0119:
The ironworks refines iron ore mined
from Mt. Coronet to make iron and
to manufacture mechanical parts.
DP.547.0120:
A sacred mountain that is capped the
year round with snow. A gigantic maze
sprawls inside it.
DP.547.0121:
There is an ancient ruin named the
Spear Pillar located here.
DP.547.0122:
A small, crescent-shaped pond that
lies inside a closed-off forest.
DP.547.0123:
A rugged, seemingly indestructible rock
mountain that is thickly blanketed by
the volcanic ash it spews.
DP.547.0124:
Inside the mountain, fissures in spilled
and hardened lava form enormous
caverns.
DP.547.0125:
A speck of an island far from any
civilization. It is covered in an
abundance of flowers.
DP.547.0126:
A small, ore-rich island that is
off the coast of Eterna City.
DP.547.0127:
Inside the island are remnants of an
iron ore mining operation from the
past.
DP.547.0128:
A small, round pond that lies
inside a closed-off forest.
DP.547.0129:
The Battle Tower--where the best of
the best Trainers gather to test
their skill--is located here.
